deep_dive.title: Measuring User's Mental Models of Speech Translation in Human-AI Collaboration
deep_dive.abstract: Millions of people use machine translation (MT) tools daily, yet little is known about their perception of what systems can and cannot do. This paper studies users' mental models of speech translation systems through a new framework based on cross-lingual question answering, where users either accept MT output or request professional re-translation to answer questions based on the information presented in a foreign language. By analyzing user behavior and accuracy trends across varying translation qualities, we examine to what extent they can predict where the system is likely to be wrong, and how this mental model evolves. Users develop stronger mental models with practice, especially when they have some knowledge of the source language, primarily by relying on surface-level error cues. Moreover, providing speech transcriptions can help users develop better mental models. Our results show the promise of cross-lingual question answering as a downstream task for studying MT mental models and advancing our understanding of human-AI collaboration.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: studies user mental models of speech translation systems with cross-lingual QA
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究用户对语音翻译系统的心理模型，使用跨语言问答
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究用户对语音翻译系统的心理模型，使用跨语言问答
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究用户对语音翻译系统的心理模型，使用跨语言问答
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a framework based on cross-lingual QA to measure users' mental models of speech translation, finding that users develop stronger models with practice and source language knowledge.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文通过跨语言问答范式研究用户对语音翻译系统的心理模型。用户需要判断是否接受机器翻译输出或请求人工重译，以评估他们预测翻译系统错误的能力。实验发现用户通过练习能形成更好的心理模型，尤其当他们对源语言有一定了解时。该研究为机器翻译人机协作设计提供了重要启示。

deep_dive.title: Do Speech Emphasis Models Generalize across Languages and Emotions?
deep_dive.abstract: Prosodic emphasis varies across languages, emotions, and speaking styles, yet existing emphasis detection models are largely trained and evaluated on monolingual neutral read speech. We introduce MMEE (Multilingual Multi-Emotion Emphasis), a corpus of 10,000 professionally recorded expressive utterances (14.13 hours) across 7 languages and 34 emotion/style categories, with three-level perceptual labels (10 annotations per sample). We benchmark two state-of-the-art architectures under monolingual, cross-lingual, multilingual, cross-emotion, cross-dataset, and data-scale settings. Monolingual models show limited zero-shot transfer, degrading across typologically distant languages, while multilingual training substantially improves robustness. Models transfer robustly between high- and low-arousal emotions; bidirectional transfer between synthetic and perceptual benchmarks suggests shared prosodic structure; and performance stays robust even at smaller training scales.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multilingual speech emphasis detection and cross-lingual evaluation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多语言语音强调检测及跨语言评估

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces MMEE, a multilingual multi-emotion emphasis corpus across 7 languages, benchmarks two architectures, and finds multilingual training improves cross-lingual generalization for prosodic emphasis detection.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对语音强调检测模型跨语言泛化问题，构建了包含7种语言34种情绪风格的多语言多情绪强调语料库MMEE，并对两种架构进行基准测试。结果表明，单语模型零样本迁移受限，而多语言训练显著提升了跨语言鲁棒性，为多语言语音处理提供了重要资源。

quick_skim.title: Scaling Audio Models Efficiently: A Joint Study of Compute Constraints and Optimization Behavior
quick_skim.abstract: In this paper, we investigate the tradeoffs between compute allocation and model performance for two speech processing tasks: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and Speech Emotion Recognition (SER). We propose a unified framework that analyzes three fundamental compute dimensions: model size ($x_N$), input length ($x_T$), and representation resolution ($x_V$). Motivated by recent advances in compute optimal scaling for multimodal models, we systematically vary these dimensions to examine their influence on task performance under fixed computational budgets. Our study provides insights into how compute resources can be optimally distributed across model capacity, temporal context, and representational granularity, offering practical guidelines for the design of efficient speech models. Through experiments on LibriSpeech and CREMA-D datasets, we demonstrate non-linear scaling behavior and identify optimal operating points. Our results show that (1) increasing model size yields diminishing returns: scaling Tiny (39M) to Small (244M) reduces WER by 8.22%, whereas Small to Medium (769M) reduces WER by only 2.35%; (2) an optimal audio duration of approximately 4 seconds exists for SER; and (3) reducing encoder token resolution provides an effective mechanism for lowering inference cost, Large-v3 (1540M) with 750 frames requires 2572 GFLOPS whereas with 1500 frames requires 5228 GFLOPS, with less than 3% relative increase in WER. Additionally, LoRA-based adaptation enables efficient finetuning with minimal performance degradation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: compute allocation for ASR and SER
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies compute allocation tradeoffs for ASR and SER across model size, input length, and representation resolution, providing practical insights for efficient audio model scaling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文系统研究了语音处理任务ASR和情感识别中计算资源分配（模型大小、输入长度、表示维度）对性能的影响，提出了在固定预算下优化分配的策略，为高效音频模型设计提供实用指导。

quick_skim.title: From Sounds to Scenes: A Benchmark for Evaluating Context-Aware Auditory Scene Understanding in Large Audio Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Recent Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) have achieved remarkable progress in audio perceptual tasks across individual acoustic layers, including speech, sound, and music. However, existing benchmarks predominantly evaluate these layers in isolation, overlooking the complex contextual relationships that arise when multiple acoustic sources co-occur in real-world auditory scenes. Real-world auditory interpretation requires Context-Aware Auditory Scene Understanding (CASU): the ability to comprehend the holistic scene by integrating sound layers. To evaluate this capability, we introduce the CASU benchmark, which assesses whether Audio LLMs can interpret auditory scenes composed of speech, acoustic events (e.g., announcements), and background environments (e.g., traffic), and reason about the logical relationships between these layers. We propose a scalable pipeline for constructing time-accurate, semi-synthetic audio streams by composing real-world scene sounds with synthetic speech. Building on this data, we design four tasks that probe scene understanding: contextual question answering, entity extraction from the scene, speaker role inference, and counterfactual reasoning where scene is manipulated. Experiments across multiple LALMs demonstrate that effective auditory scene understanding requires integration over all auditory layers, rather than reliance on speech or sound alone, underscoring the necessity of CASU for advancing complex audio understanding in LALMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for evaluating context-aware auditory scene understanding in Large Audio Language Models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估大型音频语言模型中上下文感知听觉场景理解的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估大型音频语言模型中上下文感知听觉场景理解的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估大型音频语言模型中上下文感知听觉场景理解的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CASU benchmark for evaluating Large Audio Language Models on context-aware auditory scene understanding integrating speech, sound, and music.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有基准通常孤立评估音频感知层，忽略了真实场景中多声源共存的上下文关系。本文提出CASU基准，用于评估大型音频语言模型在整合语音、声音和音乐的听觉场景理解能力。该基准有助于推动更全面的音频理解评估。

quick_skim.title: MSU-Bench: Towards Speaker-Centric Understanding in Conversational Multi-Speaker Scenarios
quick_skim.abstract: Spoken Language Understanding (SLU) is moving from task-specific pipelines toward large audio language models (LALMs) that generate natural-language responses. However, existing speech benchmarks mainly focus on single-speaker settings or isolated subtasks, leaving speaker-centric understanding in realistic multi-speaker conversations insufficiently evaluated. We introduce MSU-Bench, a diagnostic benchmark for multi-speaker conversational understanding, covering 16 speaker-centric tasks and 2,300 QA instances in a two-tier framework from speaker grounding to dialogue reasoning. We build a Gemini-assisted annotation and QA generation pipeline with human-in-the-loop verification, achieving high QA validity and strong agreement between human answers and verified labels. We further analyze speaker-referencing schemes and diagnostic error types to reveal bottlenecks in speaker grounding and reasoning. Experiments reveal clear gaps across model families, with closed-source systems leading overall but all models still facing challenges in complex speaker grounding and multi-speaker reasoning. The benchmark annotations, metadata, and evaluation scripts will be available at the GitHub repository: https://github.com/ASLP-lab/MSU-Bench.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for multi-speaker conversational speech understanding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多说话人对话语音理解基准

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MSU-Bench is a diagnostic benchmark for multi-speaker conversational understanding covering 16 speaker-centric tasks and 2,300 QA instances.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多说话人对话场景中说话人中心理解评估不足的问题，本文构建MSU-Bench诊断基准，覆盖16项说话人中心任务和2300个问答实例，基于双框架从说话人定位到对话推理。采用大型语言模型辅助标注和人机验证，实现高有效性。该基准为评估和推动多说话人语音理解提供了关键评测工具。

quick_skim.title: Adaptive Oscillatory Inductive Bias for Modeling Sharp Prosodic Dynamics in Diffusion-Based TTS
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based text-to-speech (TTS) models have achieved significant improvements in speech quality. However, modeling sharp prosodic transitions and rapid pitch variations in expressive speech remains challenging. Existing diffusion-based TTS decoders commonly utilize periodic nonlinearities such as Snake activation function to capture harmonic structures, but this activation funcation provides limited adaptability when modeling abrupt amplitude and frequency variations. In this paper, we investigate the role of oscillatory inductive bias in diffusion-based TTS decoders and introduce an adaptive oscillatory nonlinearity that enables controllable periodic modulation while maintaining signal stability through a linear bypass component. We refer the resulting TTS system as OscillaTTS. Experiments on the LJSpeech and Emotional Speech Dataset show consistent improvements across objective and subjective evaluations, indicating improved modeling of expressive prosodic dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adaptive oscillatory nonlinearity for controllable TTS modulation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自适应振荡非线性实现可控TTS调制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自适应振荡非线性实现可控TTS调制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 自适应振荡非线性实现可控TTS调制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an adaptive oscillatory nonlinearity for diffusion-based TTS that enables controllable periodic modulation to improve modeling of sharp prosodic dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对扩散TTS在表达性语音中难以建模尖锐韵律跃变的问题，本文提出一种自适应振荡非线性激活函数，通过线性旁路保持信号稳定性，实现可控周期调制。实验表明该方法能更好捕捉快速基频和幅度变化，显著提升韵律自然度。该工作为可控TTS中的韵律精细调控提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对扩散TTS在表达性语音中难以建模尖锐韵律跃变的问题，本文提出一种自适应振荡非线性激活函数，通过线性旁路保持信号稳定性，实现可控周期调制。实验表明该方法能更好捕捉快速基频和幅度变化，显著提升韵律自然度。该工作为可控TTS中的韵律精细调控提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: FlowTTS-GRPO: Online Reinforcement Learning with Multi-Objective Reward Optimization for Flow-Matching Based Text-to-Speech
quick_skim.abstract: Existing Reinforcement Learning (RL) research for Text-to-Speech (TTS) focuses on large language models (LLMs), leaving Flow-Matching (FM) under-explored. We present FlowTTS-GRPO, an online RL framework for FM-based TTS. By converting ordinary differential equation (ODE) trajectories into stochastic differential equation (SDE) paths, our method enables direct fine-tuning of open-source FM models without auxiliary models. We show that a weighted reward combination converges faster than a probabilistic scheme, and identify three practical optimizations: omitting classifier-free guidance (CFG) during training accelerates convergence; synthesizing hard cases improves robustness; and applying RL to the FM component enhances audio-detail metrics. Experiments on CosyVoice 3.0 and F5-TTS demonstrate objective and subjective preference gains in speaker similarity and perceptual quality, with F5-TTS also improving intelligibility.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 流匹配TTS的强化学习微调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 流匹配TTS的强化学习微调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 流匹配TTS的强化学习微调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FlowTTS-GRPO enables online RL fine-tuning for flow-matching TTS via ODE-to-SDE conversion, achieving faster convergence and improved robustness.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出FlowTTS-GRPO框架，将流匹配TTS的ODE轨迹转化为SDE路径，实现在线强化学习微调。通过加权奖励组合和省略引导机制，加速收敛并提升鲁棒性，为流匹配TTS引入RL提供了有效方案。

quick_skim.title: Retrieval-Grounded Multilingual LLM Assistance for Island Smallholder Farmers
quick_skim.abstract: Smallholder farming communities in remote, depopulating areas have limited access to agricultural advice, and their locally specific agronomic knowledge, often expressed in regional dialect, is poorly represented in the global corpora on which Large Language Models (LLMs) are trained. A general-purpose chatbot therefore answers fluently but unreliably, ungrounded in authoritative local data farmers can trust. This paper presents a conversational AI assistant, Falco eleonorae, embedded in a bilingual (Greek-primary, English-secondary) e-market platform serving farmers and cooperatives of a defined island area of interest. It is a thin Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) proxy in front of a geospatially-aware agronomic agent rather than a self-hosted model. Answer generation and tool selection are delegated to a managed upstream service on OpenAI GPT-5-family models, while one bounded task, describing an uploaded field photograph, is handled directly by a vision-capable model so only text reaches the agent, and voice input is transcribed by a managed EU streaming speech-to-text service. Grounding comes not from a self-hosted vector database but from tool-augmented retrieval: a Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool queries a curated, read-only, bilingual data interface exposing local crops, a seasonal calendar, traditional practices, a dialect glossary, products, agritourism experiences, cooperatives, and training content, each wrapped in a geospatial Well-Known Text envelope anchoring the agent to the area of interest. We detail its multilingual, voice, and image modalities, its progressive-web-application and accessibility design for low-bandwidth field use, and its security and data-protection posture, and argue that for a small, resource-constrained rural deployment a managed, grounded multilingual assistant is more attainable and trustworthy than a self-hosted model.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: retrieval-grounded multilingual LLM for dialectal speech processing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 检索增强的多语言LLM助手，处理方言和低资源语言

quick_skim.title: Comparative Reasoning: Making an Audio Language Model Better at Comparing Emotions
quick_skim.abstract: Large audio-language models (LALMs) can reason about audio, yet it remains unclear whether they can perform comparative judgments between two speech signals along emotional, environmental, linguistic, prosodic, and interpersonal dimensions. We study this question in the context of speech emotion recognition (SER), where the model determines which utterance exhibits higher arousal, valence, or dominance. We introduce a reasoning-guided ordinal SER framework that conditions an LALM on paired speech inputs. The model is trained using reasoning traces generated from both semantic audio descriptions and acoustic evidence derived from GeMAPS features, enabling interpretable comparative decisions. Beyond direct supervision, we also employ direct preference optimization to encourage stronger separation for emotional differences. Experiments show that the proposed framework improves preference prediction while requiring only 5% of the training data used by conventional ordinal SER systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: audio language model for comparative emotion reasoning in speech
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 音频语言模型进行语音情绪比较推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 音频语言模型进行语音情绪比较推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 音频语言模型进行语音情绪比较推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper studies comparative reasoning in audio-language models for speech emotion recognition, proposing a reasoning-guided ordinal SER framework that uses paired inputs and acoustic features, improving interpretable comparative decisions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究音频语言模型在语音情绪识别中的比较推理能力，提出一种推理引导的序数情绪识别框架。该框架将语音对输入与声学特征结合，生成可解释的比较决策，提升了模型在情绪维度上的判断能力，对多语言语音理解有参考价值。

quick_skim.title: AnySimLite: A Lightweight Few-Shot Similarity Encoder for On-Device Speech-Adjacent Classification
quick_skim.abstract: To minimize privacy concerns and inference latency on edge devices like smartphones, lightweight on-device models remain important for end-user applications. Many of these applications involve natural language classification, but deploying multiple specialized models creates a memory footprint challenge. We investigate: Can a single lightweight architecture solve multiple Speech-Adjacent (SA) classification tasks through reduction to a nuanced text similarity formulation? We propose AnySimLite, a lightweight similarity encoder that combines word-level and character-level channels. Together with a dataset transformation strategy, we evaluate AnySimLite across multiple SA classification tasks and show that it consistently achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) or SOTA-competitive performance in few-shot settings while maintaining a low memory footprint. Even in the worst case, the performance drop remains below 7% while using $<\frac{1}{250}^{\mathrm{th}}$ of the model size of the SOTA qLLaMA_LoRA-7B baseline.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: few-shot similarity encoder for speech-adjacent classification tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 少样本相似度编码器用于语音相邻分类任务
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 少样本相似度编码器用于语音相邻分类任务
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 少样本相似度编码器用于语音相邻分类任务
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AnySimLite, a lightweight similarity encoder using word and character channels, enabling a single architecture to solve multiple speech-adjacent classification tasks via few-shot similarity formulation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对设备端部署多个语音相关分类模型导致的内存占用问题，本文提出AnySimLite轻量级相似度编码器。该编码器结合词级和字符级通道，通过将多个分类任务转化为统一的文本相似度形式，实现单个模型处理多种语音相邻分类任务。实验表明其在少样本设置下具有一致的有效性。

quick_skim.title: ZONOS2 Technical Report
quick_skim.abstract: We present ZONOS2 8B, our latest TTS model, which achieves state-of-the-art naturalness, prosody, and voice cloning fidelity. We improve upon Zonos-v0.1 across scale, data, and training recipe. We scale the model from 1.6B to 8B total parameters (900M active) with a novel mixture-of-experts (MoE) backbone, improving inference latency and throughput. We expand our training corpus from 200K to over 6M hours using a new data processing pipeline, and we simplify our post-training and conditioning recipes to improve naturalness and voice cloning fidelity. We evaluate ZONOS2 8B on quality, speaker similarity, WER, and ZTTS1-Eval, our novel TTS benchmark, where it performs competitively with state-of-the-art systems while maintaining good streaming latency. We release our model weights and example inference code under an Apache 2.0 license on GitHub and Hugging Face.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ZONOS2 8B TTS model achieves SOTA naturalness and voice cloning with MoE backbone and 6M hours training data, introduces ZTTS1-Eval benchmark.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文介绍了ZONOS2 8B文本转语音模型，采用混合专家架构和600万小时训练数据，在自然度、韵律和语音克隆保真度上达到最先进水平；同时提出了ZTTS1-Eval基准用于系统评估TTS质量。模型权重已开源，为下游应用提供了强大基础。

quick_skim.title: HybridCodec: Modeling Discrete and Continuous Representations for Efficient Speech Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Discrete audio representations have become increasingly popular for building multimodal text-audio systems and integrating audio capabilities into Large Language Models (LLMs). However, numerous studies report performance degradation on various downstream tasks due to information loss during discretization. To address this, we propose a novel approach combining temporally compressed discrete tokens with dimensionality-reduced continuous residuals. Our framework consists of a hybridized discrete-continuous focal modulation codec and a hybrid Transformer. This architecture performs autoregressive inference in the discrete domain, coupled with non-autoregressive prediction and continuous residual upsampling. Experimental results show that our approach significantly improves the retention of speaker characteristics compared to discrete-only methods, while simultaneously reducing the number of required autoregressive steps.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speech codec for speech language models, enabling efficient multilingual speech synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音编解码器用于语音语言模型，支持高效多语言语音合成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语音编解码器用于语音语言模型，支持高效多语言语音合成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语音编解码器用于语音语言模型，支持高效多语言语音合成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes HybridCodec combining discrete tokens and continuous residuals for speech language models, improving representation quality and efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对离散音频表示在下游任务中的信息损失问题，提出HybridCodec，结合时间压缩的离散令牌与维度缩减的连续残差，采用混合离散-连续焦点调制编解码器和混合Transformer架构，在离散域自回归推理同时进行非自回归连续残差上采样。实验表明该方法在保持高效率的同时提升了语音表示质量，为多语言语音合成等应用提供了更优的编码方案。

quick_skim.title: ParaPairAudioBench: Paralinguistic Pairwise Audio Benchmark for LALM-as-a-Judge
quick_skim.abstract: Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) have been widely used as judge models for the automatic evaluation of generated speech. However, prior approaches predominantly focus on holistic naturalness, leaving fine-grained paralinguistic distinctions underexplored. We introduce ParaPairAudioBench, a pairwise benchmark of 5,175 audio pairs across five paralinguistic dimensions: Style, Rate, Emphasis, Age, and Gender. Our experiments show that current LALM judges still lag behind human judgments by 32%p on average and exhibit severe calibration failures, particularly in Tie cases where the correct decision is to abstain. To further analyze lexical versus acoustic reliance, the benchmark includes both same-transcript and cross-transcript conditions. ParaPairAudioBench enables multi-dimensional, calibration-aware assessment of the reliability of LALM-as-a-Judge for paralinguistic speech evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: paralinguistic audio benchmark for evaluating LALM judges of generated speech
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于评估生成语音评测模型的副语言音频基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于评估生成语音评测模型的副语言音频基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于评估生成语音评测模型的副语言音频基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ParaPairAudioBench provides 5,175 audio pairs across five paralinguistic dimensions to evaluate LALM-as-a-judge, showing judges lag human by 32%p and suffer calibration failures.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: ParaPairAudioBench提出了一个包含5,175对音频的副语言成对基准，覆盖风格、语速、强调、年龄和性别五个维度，用于评估大型音频语言模型作为评测者的能力。实验表明，当前评测模型平均落后人类32个百分点，且在平局情况下存在严重的校准失败。该基准同时包含相同文本和跨文本条件，以分析词汇与声学依赖。

quick_skim.title: HPRO: Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization via Preference Extraction for Emotional Text-to-Speech
quick_skim.abstract: Recently, Large Language Model (LLM)-based Text-to-Speech (TTS) models have achieved remarkable naturalness. However, the standard Supervised Fine-Tuning paradigm often converges to statistically averaged prosody, limiting emotional expressiveness. While preference-driven optimization offers a promising alternative, existing approaches suffer from two structural mismatches: information conflict, where content and emotion in a shared latent space produce conflicting gradients, leading to reward hacking and semantic degradation; and scale gap, where sparse sentence-level rewards struggle to guide dense frame-level generation. To overcome these challenges, we propose HPRO, a hierarchical progressive reward optimization framework. Within HPRO, we introduce the HD-Emo codec as a novel differentiable reward model to resolve the information conflict. It extracts speech into distinct content and style preference tokens, structurally isolating emotional optimization from semantic content. Building upon this structured preference space, HPRO bridges the scale gap by progressively aligning frame-, word- and sentence-level objectives. Experiments demonstrate that HPRO significantly enhances emotional expressiveness, while effectively preserving linguistic intelligibility. The code and audio samples are publicly available at https://xxh333.github.io/hpro-demo/.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HPRO introduces hierarchical progressive reward optimization for LLM-based TTS to enhance emotional expressiveness, mitigating information conflict and scale gap issues.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大模型TTS在情感表达上的不足，提出分层渐进奖励优化方法HPRO，通过解耦内容与情感空间并采用逐帧奖励引导，解决了信息冲突和尺度不匹配问题，显著提升了情感表现力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对大模型TTS在情感表达上的不足，提出分层渐进奖励优化方法HPRO，通过解耦内容与情感空间并采用逐帧奖励引导，解决了信息冲突和尺度不匹配问题，显著提升了情感表现力。

quick_skim.title: STEB: A Speech-to-Speech Translation Expressiveness Benchmark for Evaluating Beyond Translation Fidelity
quick_skim.abstract: Speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) should preserve not only lexical meaning, but also expressive attributes: emotion, scenario style (e.g., news reporting vs. dramatic dialogue), and nonverbal vocalizations (NVs). Moreover, collecting cross-lingual target speech that is both translation-faithful and expressively aligned with the source is difficult at scale, making reference-based evaluation impractical. We introduce STEB (Speech-to-Speech Translation Expressiveness Benchmark), a 32.6-hour Chinese--English benchmark that evaluates both standard dimensions (translation fidelity, speaker similarity, duration alignment) and expressiveness dimensions (emotion, scenario style, NV preservation). For expressiveness evaluation, STEB uses a caption-then-summarize framework that converts speech into structured expressive attributes and compares source and hypothesis attributes with an LLM judge. Human validation shows statistically significant correlations with listener judgments across all expressive dimensions. We evaluate six S2ST systems covering cascaded systems, end-to-end models, and speech large language models. Many systems, especially cascaded ones, achieve strong translation fidelity, but they still struggle with emotion preservation (best: 3.82/5) and NV preservation (best: 2.31/5). These results reveal a gap between semantic transfer and expressive transfer, identifying expressiveness preservation as an open challenge for S2ST. Audio samples are available at https://cmots.github.io/steb.github.io/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speech-to-speech translation benchmark evaluating expressiveness dimensions beyond fidelity
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音到语音翻译表达性基准，评估保真度之外的维度
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语音到语音翻译表达性基准，评估保真度之外的维度

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: STEB introduces a 32.6-hour Chinese-English speech-to-speech translation benchmark evaluating both translation fidelity and expressiveness dimensions (emotion, scenario style, nonverbal vocalizations), with reference-free evaluation.

quick_skim.title: SFL-MTSC: Leveraging Semantic Frame-Level Multi-Task Self-Consistency for Robust Multi-Intent Spoken Language Understanding
quick_skim.abstract: Prompt-based spoken language understanding (SLU) with large language models (LLMs) often suffers from inconsistent intent--slot structures due to decoding stochasticity, particularly in multi-intent scenarios. In view of this, we propose Semantic Frame-Level Multi-Task Self-Consistency (SFL-MTSC), a novel structured aggregation framework operating at the semantic frame level. Instead of output-level majority voting, SFL-MTSC decomposes predictions into intent-specific frames, applies domain--intent grouping and slot-level clustering, and evaluates cluster reliability using path support scoring. Reliable frames are retained and re-integrated to form the final prediction. Zero-shot experiments on the MAC-SLU benchmark dataset show improved slot F1 and overall accuracy over single-path inference, while intent accuracy remains largely stable across most settings.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-intent spoken language understanding with self-consistency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多意图口语理解中的自一致性方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多意图口语理解中的自一致性方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多意图口语理解中的自一致性方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SFL-MTSC, a semantic frame-level structured aggregation framework for robust multi-intent SLU, using intent-specific frame decomposition and slot-level clustering with path support scoring.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型在口语理解中生成不一致意图-槽结构的问题，本文提出语义帧级多任务自一致性框架SFL-MTSC。该框架将预测分解为意图特定帧，进行领域-意图分组和槽级聚类，并通过路径支持评分评估可靠性。零样本实验表明该方法显著提升了多意图场景下的鲁棒性。

quick_skim.title: RedVox: Safety and Fairness Gaps in Speech Models Across Languages
quick_skim.abstract: Speech-capable models are increasingly deployed in real-world applications across languages. Yet their safety and fairness beyond English settings and under naturalistic conditions remain understudied. We survey safety reporting practices across state-of-the-art speech model releases, finding that only 8% document any multilingual analysis. To address this gap, we introduce RedVox, a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for audio and speech built on real voices, covering unsafe and unfair stereotypical requests across five languages (English, French, Italian, Spanish, and German). Evaluating eight state-of-the-art models, we find that vulnerabilities persist even under non-adversarial conditions, worsen in non-English languages, and are amplified when the request comes from a spoken input. Finally, by surveying the participants who contributed to RedVox, we document the unique personal and privacy challenges of collecting speech data with human participants, pointing to broader sociotechnical challenges in naturalistic speech safety research.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multilingual speech safety and fairness benchmark across 5 languages
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨5种语言的多语言语音安全与公平基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨5种语言的多语言语音安全与公平基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨5种语言的多语言语音安全与公平基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces RedVox, a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for speech models across 5 languages, revealing that vulnerabilities worsen in non-English settings and are amplified under naturalistic conditions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 语音模型在非英语环境下的安全与公平性研究不足。本文提出RedVox，一个覆盖英语、法语、意大利语、西班牙语和德语的多语言语音安全与公平基准，基于真实语音构建。评估8个最新模型发现，非对抗性条件下漏洞普遍存在，非英语语言情况更糟，自然条件下问题加剧。该基准为多语言语音模型的安全审计提供了标准工具。

quick_skim.title: SHIFT: Gate-Modulated Activation Steering for Knowledge Conflict Mitigation in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhances LLMs by incorporating external knowledge to support response generation. However, conflicts between retrieved context and parametric knowledge have emerged as a critical challenge in RAG systems. To mitigate such conflicts, numerous studies have attempted to identify and edit knowledge-related internal neurons, aiming to improve the ability of LLMs to rely on contextual evidence during generation. However, these neuron-level approaches may introduce unintended cascading effects that compromise the general capabilities of LLMs, as the modified neurons are often entangled with broader model behaviors and functionalities. In this paper, we introduce SHIFT, a novel framework that reformulates neuron-level modification as learnable gate modulation, allowing LLMs to adaptively regulate internal activations for knowledge conflict resolution. Technically, our SHIFT equips LLMs with a lightweight gate module and optimizes fewer than 0.01% trainable parameters while keeping the backbone model frozen. During generation, the gate module adjusts the model's internal representations to adaptively leverage contextual and parametric knowledge. Extensive experiments on six datasets validate the effectiveness of our SHIFT in comparison with various competing baselines. All datasets and code are available at https://github.com/OpenBMB/SHIFT.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RAG framework for mitigating knowledge conflicts between retrieved context and parametric knowledge
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 缓解检索上下文与参数知识之间冲突的RAG框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 缓解检索上下文与参数知识之间冲突的RAG框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 缓解检索上下文与参数知识之间冲突的RAG框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SHIFT introduces gate-modulated activation steering to resolve knowledge conflicts in RAG, avoiding cascading effects of neuron editing while improving reliance on contextual evidence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: SHIFT提出了一种门控激活引导方法来解决检索增强生成中的知识冲突。与修改神经元的方法不同，SHIFT通过轻量级门控模块动态调整模型隐藏状态，使模型更倾向于利用检索到的上下文证据，同时保持模型的一般能力。实验表明该方法有效缓解了冲突，提升了RAG系统的鲁棒性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: SHIFT提出了一种门控激活引导方法来解决检索增强生成中的知识冲突。与修改神经元的方法不同，SHIFT通过轻量级门控模块动态调整模型隐藏状态，使模型更倾向于利用检索到的上下文证据，同时保持模型的一般能力。实验表明该方法有效缓解了冲突，提升了RAG系统的鲁棒性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 我在进行第二届中文叙实性推理评测 Factivity Inference Evaluation 2026 (FIE2026) 任务，请你帮我调研一下RAG相关的论文，用于fewshot提升性能，同时最好能和句子的语言学特征和维度相结合的论文, especially work that connects or combines: retrieval augmented generation for few shot factivity inference; few shot learning techniques in RAG for linguistic tasks; linguistic features of sentences for factuality detection; sentence factivity inference using RAG; papers on retrieval augmented generation for few shot factivity inference with linguistic sentence features; methods integrating linguistic dimensions of sentences into RAG for improved few shot performance; RAG approaches for sentence factivity detection using linguistic features; survey on combining RAG with linguistic analysis for few shot reasoning.
